stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 standalone net profit jumped 68% YoY to Rs 530 crore on 3.2% rise in net total income to Rs 3,570 crore; NIM expanded sequentially to 6.2% with management guiding 10-20 bps further expansion over next 2-3 quarters aided by deposit repricing. Stock surged ~10% on April 29 to ~Rs 197 on the earnings beat (driven by lower provisioning). Loan growth guidance 14-15%. Multiple brokerages raised targets to Rs 200-210.
stocks.sector_context: Indian private banks reporting mixed Q4 FY26: IndusInd Bank delivered turnaround (PAT Rs 594 cr vs YoY loss) with NIM 3.39%; Axis Bank PAT marginally down 0.6% to Rs 7,071 cr with NIM compression to 3.73%; ICICI Bank stable. Microfinance stress easing supports MFI-heavy lenders. Bandhan's 6.2% NIM is at the top end of the sector.
stocks.peer_comparison: Bandhan's Q4 PAT growth (+68%) and NIM (6.2%) materially outperform large private peers (Axis flat, IndusInd recovering off low base). Strongest reported earnings momentum among MFI-exposed banks this print season.
red_flags: Q4 PAT beat partly driven by lower provisioning — quality-of-earnings caveat
NII growth modest in absolute terms relative to advance growth
catalysts: NIM expansion guidance of 10-20 bps over next 2-3 quarters
Q4 FY26 earnings call audio uploaded for investors

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 net profit up 24% YoY to Rs 303 cr; revenue up 29% to Rs 2,119 cr; EBITDA up 61% to Rs 355 cr. FY26 PAT Rs 747.9 cr on revenue Rs 7,002 cr. Final dividend Rs 6.70/share recommended (payable within 30 days of 110th AGM). Stock surged up to 14% intraday on April 29 to a high of Rs 3,275; up ~49% in April alone. Antique raised target citing margin surprise and L1 status in Rs 33,000 cr next-gen corvette order to be finalised in Q1 FY27. Government confirmed no encumbrance on President of India shares.
stocks.sector_context: Defence PSU shipbuilders rallying broadly: Cochin Shipyard +47% in a month, Mazagon Dock up to +21%. FY27 defence budget of Rs 7.85 lakh cr (+15.2%) with capex up ~22% supports order pipeline. Geopolitical premium from Middle East tensions adding to sentiment. Sector seeing strong execution momentum into Q4 FY26.
stocks.peer_comparison: GRSE's Q4 margin beat is best-in-class among shipyard PSUs; ranks alongside Cochin Shipyard as a top performer in April. Order book visibility strong with potential Rs 33,000 cr corvette win pending in Q1 FY27.
red_flags: Stock up ~49% in April alone — significant near-term overextension; profit-booking risk after the Q4 print
Valuation at multi-year highs prices in continued large-order wins
catalysts: Next-gen corvette order finalisation in Q1 FY27 (~Rs 33,000 cr)
Sustained defence capex execution through FY27

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (reported April 28): revenue up 28.21% YoY to Rs 52,462.5 cr but net profit fell 6.45% YoY to Rs 3,659 cr due to mark-to-market impact and cost pressure on margins. Highest-ever quarterly sales of 676,209 units (+11.8% YoY); exports record at 137,215. FY26 PAT all-time high Rs 14,445.4 cr; FY26 sales record 24.2 lakh units. Highest-ever dividend of Rs 140/share recommended (vs Rs 135 prior). Stock fell ~2% to Rs 12,953 post-results.
stocks.sector_context: Auto sector recovering after 13% correction in March 2026 from West Asia conflict supply-chain fears; BSE Auto rose 6.57% on April 8. 2026 sector growth outlook 6-8%; April 1 price hikes (0.5-3%) absorbing input cost inflation. CLSA earlier flagged 15% correction risk for PVs (Tata Motors PV, M&M, TVS). Rupee depreciation a tailwind for exporters.
stocks.peer_comparison: Maruti's volume strength (record sales, record exports) outpaces peers but margin compression echoes sector-wide cost concerns. Tata Motors CV grew 25% YoY in Q4. Maruti remains the most defensive PV name vs Tata/M&M but earnings quality this quarter weaker than topline suggests.
red_flags: Q4 net profit decline of 6.45% YoY despite strong topline — margin compression theme
Mark-to-market hit suggests treasury/investment portfolio drag
Stock weak post-results (-2%) signaling earnings disappointment
catalysts: e Vitara and Victorious model ramp-up

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results scheduled for board approval today, April 29, after market hours; analyst call April 30 at 4:30 PM. Pre-released Q4 business update showed AUM up 22% YoY to Rs 5.10 lakh cr and new loans booked up 20.5% YoY to Rs 1.28 lakh cr. Brokerage estimates: PAT Rs 5,581-5,701 cr (+22.8% to +25.4% YoY); NII Rs 11,832-11,896 cr (+20.7% to +21.3% YoY). Dividend recommendation expected. Stock up ~15% in past month but still down 4.63% YTD 2026.
stocks.sector_context: NBFC/financials sector benefitting from steady credit demand; Bajaj Finance is the bellwether for retail lending. Recent rally suggests positioning ahead of results; Bajaj Finserv parent also reports around same window.
stocks.peer_comparison: AUM growth of 22% remains best-in-class among large NBFCs; loan growth pace holds vs deceleration concerns flagged earlier in cycle. Pre-results positioning is strong — bar is high.
red_flags: Event risk: results released today after-hours — high single-day volatility around print
Stock rallied ~15% in a month into the print — high expectations bar; any miss likely punished
Still negative YTD 2026 — overhang from prior-quarter concerns persists
catalysts: Q4 FY26 PAT/NII/NIM/asset quality print today (April 29)
April 30 analyst call commentary on FY27 guidance

stocks.recent_news: April 29 is the LAST day to buy Vedanta shares to qualify for the 5-way demerger (T+1 settlement). April 30 is ex-date with a special pre-open price discovery session 9:15-9:45 AM, normal trading from 10 AM. Record date May 1. Shareholders receive 1:1 in 4 new entities (Vedanta Aluminium, Oil & Gas, Power, Steel & Iron) plus residual VEDL. Q4 FY26 results also today; third interim dividend of Rs 11/share (1100%) recommended. Stock up ~45% in 6 months; ~Rs 739 on April 28; market cap ~Rs 2.89 lakh cr.
stocks.sector_context: Metals sector mixed; Vedanta is event-driven today rather than sector-driven. Hindustan Zinc and other metals peers also have corporate actions this week. Demerger value-unlock theme priced into stock.
stocks.peer_comparison: Vedanta substantially outperforming Hindalco, NALCO, and Hindustan Zinc on demerger catalyst. Direct peer comparison less meaningful until the new entities list. Sum-of-parts post-demerger valuation is the key debate.
red_flags: EXTREME event risk: ex-date tomorrow April 30 with special price discovery session — share price will mechanically reset to reflect demerger value transfer
Corporate-action volatility makes intraday/swing trades on April 29 highly risky for algo systems not designed for ex-date adjustments
After +45% in 6 months, stock priced for successful execution; SOTP compression risk if individual entities trade weaker than expected
Promoter group leverage concerns at parent level persist
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results today with Rs 11/share third interim dividend recommendation
Demerger ex-date April 30 with special price discovery session
Listing of 4 new demerged entities by May 2026
